steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence: "Artemisia Gentileschi's painting La Pittura, symbolically ______ to portray the power of female artists..., is a highly regarded example of allegory." The blank sits inside a comma-set modifier that describes the noun "painting." A noun modifier set off by commas must use a participle, not a finite verb.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — has functioned: A present-perfect finite verb cannot serve as a participial modifier inside a noun phrase. Using it here creates a grammatically broken sentence. ✗
  • Choice B — was functioning: A past-progressive finite verb has the same problem — it needs its own subject and cannot modify "painting" within a comma-set phrase. ✗
  • Choice C — functions: A simple present finite verb also cannot function as a participial modifier; placing it here conflicts with the main verb "is" and produces an ungrammatical structure. ✗
  • Choice D — functioning: The present participle "functioning" correctly acts as a modifier, creating the participial phrase "symbolically functioning to portray the power of female artists," which describes "painting" without conflicting with the main verb. ✓
D is correct.
